results.title: EOG Resources AI Adoption Tracker | Larridin | AI Initiative Tracker
highlights:
EOG Resources has established itself as a pioneering force in AI adoption within the oil and gas industry, transitioning from early experimentation to operational deployment across multiple business functions. The company's distinctive approach centers on developing proprietary AI systems rather than relying solely on external partners, creating what analysts call a powerful 'data-to-decision flywheel' [1]. EOG's AI strategy is built on three core pillars: predictive maintenance for equipment optimization, drilling optimization through real-time analytics, and reservoir management using machine learning algorithms to identify productive zones [2]. The company has developed over 100 in-house applications giving every employee a 'virtual control room,' demonstrating its commitment to becoming what industry experts term an 'AI-native E&P company' [3]. EOG's unique philosophy of 'Owning Data from Creation to Delivery' has enabled the creation of massive proprietary data warehouses that fuel sophisticated AI models, positioning the company to achieve its stated goal of a 5% increase in efficiency per employee [4].
...
## AI Initiatives
...
### 100+ Proprietary AI Applications
...
Comprehensive suite of in-house AI applications providing virtual control room capabilities
...
### AI-Driven Well Forecasting
...
Machine learning models for production forecasting and well performance prediction
...
### Predictive Maintenance System
...
AI-powered system using machine learning algorithms to analyze historical equipment data and predict maintenance needs
...
monitors drilling rigs and critical equipment to identify
...
### AI-Enhanced Drilling Optimization
...
Advanced algorithms analyze geological data and drilling parameters to optimize well placement and drilling techniques
...
### Machine Learning Reservoir Management
...
AI models analyze seismic data, reservoir characteristics, and production history to identify productive zones
...
EOG uses AI-driven algorithms to analyze geological data and drilling parameters in real-time, optimizing well placement and drilling techniques. This includes predictive maintenance systems that monitor equipment health and identify potential failures before they occur, reducing downtime and costs.
...
EOG focuses on developing proprietary AI systems in-house rather than relying primarily on external partners. The company has built over 100 custom AI applications and maintains a philosophy of 'Owning Data from Creation to Delivery,' creating a self-reinforcing data-to-decision flywheel.
...
EOG reports improved operational efficiency, reduced drilling time and costs, enhanced safety protocols, better reservoir management, and more accurate production forecasting. The company targets a 5% increase in efficiency per employee through AI deployment.
...
EOG employs machine learning models to analyze seismic data, reservoir characteristics, and production history to identify the most productive zones within reservoirs. This enables maximized oil recovery while minimizing environmental impact through targeted recovery strategies.
...
Data is central to EOG's AI strategy, with the company collecting information from over 5,000 wells across U.S. unconventional plays. EOG has developed massive proprietary data warehouses and uses 20 mobile apps for real-time data reporting, creating unique datasets that fuel their AI models.
...
| Application | Vendor | Use Case |
...
| --- | --- | --- |
| IFS Merrick – Carte | IFS | Analytics and Business Intelligence for data visualization and decision support |
...
| Microsoft Azure AI Services | Microsoft | Cloud-based AI and machine learning model hosting and deployment |
| NetBrain Network Automation | NetBrain | Network topology mapping and automated discovery of configuration problems |
| Proprietary AI Suite | In-house development | 100+ custom applications for drilling optimization, predictive maintenance, and reservoir management |

results.title: Himanshu S.
highlights:
Digital Upstream Engineer | Exploration Platform | Real-Time Geo-steering & Rock Analysis | Bridging Subsurface Science into Scalable Digital Solutions
...
Senior Data Engineer at [EOG Resources](https://www.linkedin.com/company/eog-resources)
...
Senior data and backend engineer who designs and ships end-to-end data platforms, APIs, and distributed systems across cloud and on-prem environments. At EOG, I'm the sole architect of an in-house platform that replaces commercial geoscience software (OpenSpirit, Halliburton EDM) with faster, automation-friendly services — and I've modernized legacy desktop apps into a Python/FastAPI + React platform, made authentication ~25x faster with Redis, and led a 200+-deployment Kubernetes migration. I care about system design, data integration, ETL/orchestration, observability-driven optimization, and modernizing legacy systems. Always happy to talk distributed data platforms. Core: Python · FastAPI · SQL · Kubernetes · Airflow · Redis · Elasticsearch · MongoDB · Oracle · SingleStore · AWS · Datadog
...
### Senior Data Engineer at [EOG Resources](https://www.linkedin.com/company/eog-resources) (Current)
...
Senior Data Engineer @ EOG’s Exploration group, focused on building and evolving internal platforms that support exploration initiatives, subsurface analysis, and data workflows.
...
- Sole architect of an in-house geoscience data-integration platform — wraps Petra & Kingdom SDKs to replace commercial software (OpenSpirit, Halliburton EDM); projected to eliminate six-figure annual licensing.
...
- Designed a hybrid orchestration system: a FastAPI gateway on Kubernetes plus on-prem Windows workers with atomic job claim, lease-based recovery, cancellation, and run-level structured logging. Tech focus: Distributed backend services, geospatial data, event-driven pipelines, scalable data models, and hybrid on-prem & cloud architectures
...
### Senior Python Engineer at EOG Resources
...
Senior Python Engineer (Contract via WebCreek) Led development of data infrastructure and service layer of a next-generation geoscience platform, replacing legacy systems with modern technologies and services.
...
- Consolidated 3 legacy C#/WPF desktop apps (geosteering interpretation, formation analysis, well-log/curve management) into a unified Python/FastAPI backend powering a new React web app (70+ REST endpoints).
...
- Built a Redis auth/RBAC cache that cut ~900 ms of per-request auth overhead to ~35 ms — protected-endpoint latency down ~75% and OIDC-server traffic ~90%.
- Built Airflow DAGs, custom plugins, and event-driven workflows syncing data many-to-many across third-party sources, internal warehouses, and app datastores.
...
- Drove a 200+-deployment Kubernetes migration (80+ services to a new cluster in ~2 months) with CI/CD updates and a no-downtime namespace/DNS cutover.
- Established quality + observability: blocking GitHub Actions CI and Datadog APM dashboards/alerts catching issues before users report them.

EOG Resources is one of the largest American companies in crude oil and natural gas exploration and production. Over the years, EOG has built a reputation for its unique culture and a commitment to returns, best-in-class exploration, technology leadership, collaborative, multi-disciplinary innovation, and responsible operations.
...
EOG opened a new office in the Kingdom to support their joint venture with Bapco Energies to explore and develop an onshore horizontal tight gas prospect. The company expects to begin drilling in 2025 and is optimistic the collaboration is just the beginning of a long-term partnership with Bapco and presence in Bahrain. The project aligns with Bahrain’s National Energy Strategy and is poised to contribute to the country’s sustainable economic growth by leveraging strategic partnerships and innovative technologies.
...
Bapco Energies and EOG Resources , a leading U.S.-based hydrocarbon exploration company, have signed a landmark agreement to evaluate a promising gas exploration prospect in the Kingdom. The signing ceremony builds on the high-level discussions held in early January 2025 where His Highness Shaikh Nasser bin Hamad Al Khalifa, His Majesty’s Representative for Humanitarian Works and Youth Affairs, Chairman of Bapco Energies met with an EOG delegation to explore opportunities in hydrocarbon exploration and innovative energy solutions.

### https://www.linkedin.com/posts/brickee_agenticai-aiagents-openclaw-activity-7436574242571325440-pjV1
...
Activity: Jacob Nguyen liked this
...
My AI assistant now reads papers, scans X, writes experiment code, and runs 24/7 — on a $24 server.
...
Over the past few weeks I set up OpenClaw — a self-hosted AI agent that lives in Telegram and quietly handles parts of my research workflow.

It currently does things like:
...
• Daily arXiv + HuggingFace paper digests
• Twitter/X and Reddit summaries for ML news
• Image generation + speech-to-text
• Web browsing and automation
• Writing experiment code for my research
...
Everything runs on a single AWS Lightsail instance (2 vCPU, 4GB RAM).
...
No SaaS.
...
No dashboards.
...
Just one small server and a chat interface.
...
Most of the system was installed using Claude Code, with a surprisingly simple setup:
...
JSON configs + Markdown “skills”
...
One design choice that made it reliable was using a multi-model cascade:
...
GPT-5.3 Codex → GLM-5 → Kimi → Claude
...
If one provider hits a rate limit or goes down, the agent automatically falls back to the next model — so it basically never stops working.
...
The biggest takeaway from building this:
...
The infrastructure for personal AI systems already exists.
...
RSS feeds for papers.
...
Cheap model APIs.
...
One small cloud server.
...
The rest is just wiring the pieces together. #AgenticAI #AIAgents #OpenClaw

results.title: EOG Data Engineer Interview Questions & Guide 2026
highlights:
### What is a Data Engineer at EOG?
...
At **EOG**, data is the foundation of strategic decision-making and operational efficiency. As a **Data Engineer**, you will play a critical role in building, optimizing, and maintaining the data pipelines that power one of the most technologically advanced independent oil and gas companies in the world. From real-time drilling telemetry and IoT sensor data to complex financial and geological datasets, your work directly impacts how **EOG** explores, develops, and produces energy assets.
...
You will join a collaborative technology environment where your solutions directly support geoscientists, reservoir engineers, and business leaders. The challenges you will tackle involve scaling data infrastructure to handle massive volumes of structured and unstructured data, migrating legacy workflows to modern cloud architectures, and ensuring high availability for critical business applications.
...
This role is ideal for engineers who want to see the tangible real-world impact of their code. At **EOG**, technology is not just a support function; it is a core competitive advantage. You will have the opportunity to work with cutting-edge data tools, solve complex data integration challenges, and drive initiatives that directly influence the company's bottom line.
...
As a **Data Engineer** at **EOG**, your daily work will sit at the intersection of software engineering, data architecture, and business operations. You will be responsible for ensuring that clean, reliable data is delivered to the right places at the right time.
...
* Designing, building, and maintaining robust data pipelines to ingest data from diverse sources, including SCADA systems, external vendor APIs, and internal transactional databases.
...
* Collaborating directly with business analysts, geoscientists, and engineering teams to understand their data needs and translate them into scalable technical solutions.
...
* Optimizing data workflows and database queries to support high-performance business applications and reporting tools.
...
* Assisting in the modernization of data infrastructure, including migrating on-premises workflows to secure, scalable cloud environments.
...
To be competitive for the **Data Engineer** position at **EOG**, you should possess a strong foundation in computer science or management information systems, combined with practical, hands-on project experience.
...
* **Must-have technical skills** – Strong proficiency in SQL (writing complex queries, joins, and indexing), basic programming skills in Python for data manipulation, and a solid understanding of relational database design principles.
...
* **Nice-to-have skills** – Familiarity with cloud platforms (such as AWS, Azure, or Google Cloud), exposure to ETL/ELT orchestration tools, and basic knowledge of data warehousing concepts.
...
* **Experience level** – For entry-level positions (Data Engineer I), a Bachelor's degree in Computer Science, MIS, Data Science, or a related field is typical,
...
A **Data Engineer** role at **EOG** offers an exceptional opportunity to apply modern data technologies to complex, high-impact challenges in the energy sector. By building robust, scalable data pipelines, you will directly empower teams of geologists, engineers, and business leaders to make critical, data-driven decisions. The collaborative culture, excellent benefits, and focus on technological innovation make **EOG** a premier place to build a career in data engineering.
...
[Systems Engineer](https://dataford.io/interview-guides/eog/systems-engineer)[Account Executive](https://dataford.io/interview-guides/eog/account-executive)[Data Scientist](https://dataford.io/interview-guides/eog/data-scientist)[Software Engineer](https://dataford.io/interview-guides/eog/software-engineer)[Financial Analyst](https://dataford.io/interview-guides/eog/financial-analyst)[Business Analyst](https://dataford.io/interview-guides/eog/business-analyst)

I design and deliver in-house software solutions for the Production side of Oil & Gas, focused on real-time data integration and control. My work bridges SCADA platforms with enterprise operations by building high-throughput, bidirectional pipelines—streaming millions of SCADA tags that represent field I/O from PLCs, RTUs, and DCS systems for analysis, while also enabling secure, and highly scalable, setpoint command workflows back to the field. I specialize in .NET service architectures, leveraging technologies such as OPC UA, MQTT, gRPC, and REST APIs to move and transform production data at scale. My solutions integrate with enterprise platforms ensuring secure, reliable, and performant data delivery across thousands of wells and field assets. My focus is on building resilient, scalable systems that keep production data flowing seamlessly—so operators, engineers, and business leaders can act on the right information at the right time.

We are seeking a highly skilled AI/ML Engineer to develop advanced AI and ML solutions for the energy and utility sector. The role requires collaboration with cross-functional teams to design, build, and deploy scalable machine learning systems.

We are currently looking for an AI / ML Engineer in the United States.This role sits at the intersection of machine learning engineering, AI systems design, and large-scale data-driven decision-making, focused on solving complex challenges in the energy and utility sector. You will contribute to the development of advanced AI and ML solutions that combine predictive modeling, optimization, and generative AI to support critical infrastructure and sustainability goals. Working closely with data scientists and software engineers, you will help design, build, and deploy scalable machine learning systems that power real-world applications. The environment is highly collaborative and innovation-driven, with a strong emphasis on applied AI, production readiness, and measurable impact. You will work across diverse use cases including forecasting, geospatial analysis, and intelligent decision systems. This role focuses on advanced analytics, experimentation, and applied AI.
...
- Independently lead end-to-end analytics and data science projects, from problem framing through delivery of actionable insights.

- 4+ years of experience writing and refining complex SQL queries against large, structured datasets.
- 3+ years of experience partnering directly with business stakeholders to translate analytical findings into actionable recommendations.
- 2+ years of experience working in a modern analytics platform (e.g., Databricks, Spark/PySpark, or similar).
- 1+ years of hands-on‑ experience applying generative AI technologies (e.g., LLM APIs, task‑specific GenAI workflows) in an enterprise analytics environment.

# Associate Data Scientist, New College Grad - 2026
...
- RRS Group is seeking a motivated and analytical Associate Data Scientist to join our team as part of the 2026 New College Graduate hiring program. This role is ideal for candidates with a strong foundation in data analysis, machine learning, statistical modeling, and Generative AI applications. The Associate Data Scientist will work closely with cross-functional teams to analyze large datasets, develop predictive models, generate actionable insights, and support data-driven business decisions.
...
- The ideal candidate demonstrates technical proficiency in SQL and Python, hands-on experience with machine learning and analytics tools, and familiarity with modern AI-assisted workflows and responsible AI practices.
...
- Develop, evaluate, and maintain descriptive and predictive machine learning models using tools such as scikit-learn, Jupyter Notebooks, Python, R, and/or SAS.
...
- Utilize Generative AI and AI-assisted tools, including LLMs, coding assistants, and AutoML platforms, to improve analytical workflows and insight generation.
...
- Apply Generative AI techniques such as prompt engineering, text summarization, classification, and LLM-assisted analysis in business or research applications.
...
- Follow responsible AI principles, including awareness of data privacy, bias mitigation, ethical AI usage, and model limitations.
...
- Bachelor’s or Master’s degree in Data Science, Computer Science, Statistics, Mathematics, Engineering, Economics, or a related quantitative field, with graduation expected in 2026.
...
- Minimum of 2 years of experience in data analysis, quantitative modeling, or data-driven decision-making through academic, internship, research, or professional experience.
...
- Strong understanding of statistics and machine learning fundamentals, including model evaluation techniques.
...
- Hands-on experience with machine learning libraries and tools such as scikit-learn, Jupyter Notebooks, Python, R, and/or SAS.
...
- Exposure to Generative AI technologies and AI-assisted analytical workflows.
...
- Experience with cloud-based analytics or machine learning platforms.
- Coursework or project experience involving NLP, LLMs, or AI-driven analytics.
- Knowledge of responsible AI practices, governance, and ethical AI considerations.
- Experience developing dashboards
...
- Programming Languages: Python, SQL, R, SAS
- Data & ML Tools: scikit-learn, Spark, Jupyter Notebooks
- Visualization Tools: Tableau, Power BI
- AI/GenAI Tools: LLMs, AutoML, coding assistants
- Statistical Techniques: Regression, classification, clustering, decision trees, predictive modeling
...
- Strong attention to detail and data quality
- Ability to manage multiple projects in a fast-paced environment
- Effective collaboration and communication skills
- Curiosity for emerging AI and data science technologies
- Commitment to ethical and responsible AI practices
